THE GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES
Human Capital Management
Our people are our greatest asset. We believe that a major strength and principal reason for our success is the quality, dedication, determination and collaboration of our people, which enables us to serve our clients, generate long-term value for our shareholders and contribute to the broader community.
Our Workforce
Our goal is to attract, retain, and promote an exceptionally skilled workforce. We invest heavily in developing and supporting our people throughout their careers, and we strive to maintain a work environment that fosters professionalism, excellence, high standards of business ethics, teamwork and cooperation among our employees worldwide.
We believe that the diversity of our workforce, including diversity of perspectives, enhances our performance-based culture and is critical to our commercial success. We previously set forth five-year aspirational hiring and representation goals which expire in 2025, and we are proud of the progress we have made. We remain focused on the importance of attracting and retaining diverse exceptional talent. In that connection, we will continue to develop programs consistent with our fundamental commitment  to inclusive merit-based promotion and in compliance with the law.
Talent Development and Retention
We seek to help our people achieve their full potential by investing in them and supporting a culture of continuous development. Our goals are to maximize individual capabilities, increase commercial effectiveness and innovation, reinforce our culture, expand professional opportunities, and help our people contribute positively to their communities. As of December 2024, the average tenure of the members of our Management Committee was approximately 24 years, and that of all of our employees was approximately 6 years. In addition, more than 40% of our partners were campus hires.
Instilling our culture in all employees is a continuous process, in which training plays an important part. We offer our employees the opportunity to participate in ongoing educational offerings and periodic seminars facilitated by our Learning & Engagement team. To accelerate their integration into the firm and our culture, new hires have the opportunity to receive training as soon as they start working via orientation programs that emphasize culture and networking, and nearly all employees participate in at least one training event each year. For our more senior employees, we provide guidance and training on how to manage people and projects effectively, exhibit strong leadership and exemplify our culture. We are also focused on developing a high performing, diverse leadership pipeline and career planning for our next generation of leaders. We maintain a variety of programs aimed at employees' professional growth and leadership development. For example, we are focused on ensuring that vice presidents have the necessary coaching, sponsorship and advocacy to support their career trajectories and strengthen their leadership platforms. Many other career development initiatives are aimed at fostering talent at the analyst and associate levels. We also work closely with our leadership teams to promote diversity and inclusion. Our global and regional Inclusion Networks and Interest Forums are open to all of our professionals to promote and advance these goals.
Enhancing our people's experience of internal mobility is a key focus, as we believe that this will inspire employees, help retain top talent and create diverse experiences to build future leaders.
Another important part of instilling our culture is our employee performance review process. Employees are reviewed by supervisors, co-workers and employees whom they supervise in a 360-degree review process that is integral to our team approach and includes an evaluation of an employee's performance. Our approach to evaluating employee performance centers on providing robust, timely and actionable feedback that facilitates professional development. We have directed our managers, as leaders at the firm, to take an active coaching role with their teams. We also engage in "The Three Conversations at GS" through which managers establish goals with their team members at the start of the year, check in mid-year on progress and then close out the year with a conversation on performance against goals.

THE GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES
We believe that our people value opportunities to contribute to their communities and that these opportunities enhance their job satisfaction. We also believe that being able to volunteer together with colleagues and support community organizations through completing local service projects strengthens our people's bond with us. Community TeamWorks, our signature volunteering initiative, enables our people to participate in high-impact, team-based volunteer opportunities, including projects coordinated with hundreds of nonprofit partner organizations worldwide. During 2024, our people volunteered approximately 103,000 hours of service globally through Community TeamWorks, with approximately 19,000 employees partnering with 660 nonprofit organizations on approximately 1,400 community projects.
Wellness
We recognize that for our people to be successful in the workplace they need support in their personal, as well as their professional, lives and that is why our wellness framework is designed to promote health and fitness, resilience, and work-life balance. We provide a number of policies for our employees that support taking time away from the office when needed, including a minimum of 20 weeks of parental leave and up to four weeks of family care leave in order to assist with the care of family members with a serious health condition, death of an immediate family member or miscarriage, in addition to bereavement leave. We allow managing directors to take time off without a fixed vacation day entitlement, and have also set a minimum annual expected vacation usage of 15 days for all employees. For longer-tenured employees, we offer an unpaid sabbatical leave.
We also continue to advance our resilience programs, offering our people a range of counseling, coaching, medical advisory and personal wellness services. We have introduced and globally scaled the internationally recognized Mental Health First Aid certification to our people. As of December 2024, over 1,300 employees were certified across the firm, surpassing our goal to train 1,000 Mental Health First Aiders by the end of 2024. We have evolved and strengthened virtual offerings to enhance access to support, with the aim of maintaining the physical and mental well-being of our people, and enhancing their effectiveness and productivity. We also launched a manager mental health training to help support leaders globally, achieving a completion rate of approximately 85%.
We understand the crucial role caregiving plays in the lives of our employees. To help enable employees to better balance their roles at work and their responsibilities at home, we offer a variety of family-centered benefits, including adoption and surrogacy stipends, as well as adult or childcare options to help our people navigate caregiving across various life stages. Employees also have access to our family care coordination services for comprehensive support and coaching across the caregiving continuum—from their transition to new parenthood to care for adult family members.

**Quantitative Report: Goldman Sachs (GS) as of 2025-04-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue & Earnings:** Exhibited strong recent growth. Q4 2024 revenue was 13.87B, a QoQ increase of 9.2% and a YoY increase of 22.5%. Q4 2024 diluted EPS was 11.9, a QoQ increase of 41.7% and a YoY increase of 117.2%. TTM revenue was 53.51B, with TTM net income of 14.28B.
- **Margins:** Profitability has expanded. The latest operating margin was 0.344, and net margin was 0.267. Operating income rose to 5.26B in Q4 2024 from 2.25B a year prior.
- **Cash Flow:** No quarterly cash flow data is available in the supplied evidence.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total equity grew steadily from 117.51B (Q1 2023) to 122.00B (Q4 2024). Total assets stood at 1675.97B, resulting in an assets/equity leverage ratio of 13.74x.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Assessment is limited without cash flow statements. Net income growth is strong and consistent with operating income trends, but the absence of cash flow data prevents analysis of accruals or cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet is characterized by high leverage (assets/equity of 13.74x). While total equity has grown, this high leverage is a primary quantitative risk factor, amplifying the impact of asset value fluctuations. Specific regulatory capital ratios are not provided.

**3. Valuation Versus History**
- **Valuation Ratios (as of 2025-03-31):** PE (diluted) = 13.475, Price-to-Book = 1.494, Price-to-Sales = 3.406, Earnings Yield = 7.4%.
- **Historical Context (12-month window):** The current PE of 13.475 is at the low end of its observed range of 12.9 to 18.9 over the past year. In contrast, Price-to-Book at 1.494 is in the middle of its 1.2 to 1.8 range. The PE has compressed significantly from 18.08 in April 2024, primarily driven by rising earnings.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
- **Price & Returns:** The stock closed at 546.29 on 2025-03-31. Over the preceding ~252 trading days, it returned +31.6%. However, it has declined -6.0% over the most recent quarterly period (~63 trading days, from 2024-12-26). The 52-week price range was 387.12 to 672.19.
- **Fundamental Correlation:** The strong annual price return (+31.6%) aligns with the period of significant earnings growth (e.g., YoY EPS growth of 117.2% in the latest quarter). The recent quarterly price decline (-6.0%) occurred alongside continued fundamental growth, suggesting a potential re-rating of valuation multiples.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** 1) **Valuation Re-rating Potential:** The PE multiple is currently compressed toward the bottom of its recent range (13.475 vs. high of 18.9) despite robust earnings growth. 2) **Earnings Momentum:** TTM operating income of 18.40B and a current operating margin of 0.344 show strong profitability, which could support the stock if sustained.
- **Downside:** 1) **Leverage Risk:** The assets/equity leverage of 13.74x creates substantial financial risk. 2) **Earnings Durability Uncertainty:** The absence of cash flow data limits visibility into the quality and sustainability of reported earnings. 3) **Price Momentum:** The recent -6.0% quarterly return indicates potential negative momentum.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative profile presents a conflict. On one hand, GS shows powerful earnings growth and a valuation (PE of 13.475) that appears low relative to its own recent history. On the other hand, the balance sheet is highly leveraged, and the absence of cash flow data introduces material uncertainty about earnings quality. The strong annual price return (+31.6%) has been partially retraced in the last quarter (-6.0%). The risk/reward is therefore conditional: attractive if earnings growth is durable and cash-generative, but risky due to leverage and incomplete cash flow evidence. A definitive conclusion is precluded by the lack of supplied cash flow data.
GS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Goldman Sachs (GS)**
**Decision Date:** 2025-04-01

### 1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative
Based on the 2024 10-K (filed 2025-02-27), Goldman Sachs operates as a leading global financial institution across three segments: **Global Banking & Markets** (investment banking, FICC, equities intermediation), **Asset & Wealth Management** (management fees, investments, private banking), and **Platform Solutions** (consumer platforms, transaction banking). The firm emphasizes its "One Goldman Sachs" initiative to deliver integrated services. Its core franchise strength lies in institutional client relationships, market-making, and advisory services. The **Platform Solutions** segment includes consumer platforms like the Apple Card, though the firm notes it has **narrowed its focus on consumer-related activities** and is transitioning the GM credit card program (expected completion Q3 2025).

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)
*   **Regulatory & Legal:**
    *   **2025-03-07:** FHFA announced a settlement with Goldman Sachs related to Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac (specifics not detailed).
    *   **2025-03-09:** The Trump administration upheld the Biden-era merger guidelines, maintaining a strict regulatory approach to M&A. The text notes this is a "setback" for banks like Goldman Sachs seeking a more relaxed environment for advisory revenue.
    *   **2025-02-24:** A Reuters article discussed Goldman Sachs being cited in recent securities class action litigation.
*   **Market & Economic Environment:**
    *   **2025-03-10 & 2025-03-04:** News reports cited Goldman Sachs stock plunging alongside other banks due to recession fears and concerns that tariffs could slow consumer spending and increase loan defaults.
    *   **2025-03-16:** Analysis highlighted President Trump's tariff stance as a real market risk, not just rhetoric.
*   **Strategic & Advisory Activity:**
    *   **2025-03-26:** Goldman Sachs acted as a stabilization manager for a EUR bond issue by Ontex.
    *   **2025-03-25:** Goldman Sachs was involved in Fleetio's $450M+ Series D and acquisition of Auto Integrate (implied by lead underwriter role in similar deals, though not explicitly stated for this transaction).
    *   **2025-02-25:** Chegg engaged Goldman Sachs to consider strategic options, including going private.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Regulatory Headwinds:** The continuation of strict M&A guidelines (2025-03-09) could constrain the advisory revenue pipeline, a key business for Global Banking & Markets.
    *   **Macroeconomic Sensitivity:** Bank stocks (including GS) are shown to be highly sensitive to recession fears and trade policy risks (tariffs), which can depress valuations and market activity (2025-03-10, 2025-03-04).
    *   **Legal & Reputational:** The firm is subject to ongoing legal actions (2025-02-24) and has reached a settlement with the FHFA (2025-03-07). Shareholder proposals regarding DEI programs present reputational considerations (2025-02-16).
    *   **Consumer Segment Execution:** The narrative confirms the strategic withdrawal from consumer banking ("strategic blunder" per 2025-03-01 summary), with ongoing program transitions (GM card). Execution risk remains during this wind-down.

*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Core Franchise Strength:** The 10-K and multiple articles (2025-03-01, 2025-03-02) reaffirm Goldman Sachs' leadership in M&A, equities, and asset management as key differentiators.
    *   **Active Advisory Mandates:** Evidence of continued deal flow, such as acting as stabilization manager (2025-03-26) and engaging in strategic reviews for companies like Chegg (2025-02-25).
    *   **Favorable Outlook (Per Firm):** Web context notes Goldman Sachs Research forecasts solid 2025 global GDP growth (2.7% YoY) and expects a healthy, micro-driven environment for equities (2025-02-21, 2025-03-05).

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral / Slightly Negative**

The franchise's core strengths in institutional markets and advisory are confirmed. However, the near-term narrative is dominated by significant headwinds: **upheld strict merger regulations** (2025-03-09) likely cap advisory revenue potential, while **macroeconomic and tariff-related volatility** (2025-03-04, 2025-03-10, 2025-03-16) pose a threat to market-making and investment banking activity. The firm is also managing legal settlements (2025-03-07) and the strategic exit from consumer platforms. While the long-term positioning in core businesses remains sound, the evidence points to a challenging operating environment for near-term performance.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Goldman Sachs presents a mixed picture: strong earnings momentum and historically compressed valuation (PE 13.5 vs. 12-month range of 12.9-18.9) are offset by high leverage (13.7x assets/equity), macro headwinds from tariff uncertainty, and strict M&A regulatory environment that could constrain advisory revenue.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Goldman Sachs presents a mixed picture: strong earnings momentum and historically compressed valuation (PE 13.5 vs. 12-month range of 12.9-18.9) are offset by high leverage (13.7x assets/equity), macro headwinds from tariff uncertainty, and strict M&A regulatory environment that could constrain advisory revenue.
key_factors: Earnings momentum: Q4 2024 EPS of $11.90 (+117% YoY) with TTM net income of $14.28B and operating margin of 34.4%, yet the stock trades at a PE of 13.5 near the bottom of its 12-month range (12.9-18.9), suggesting potential re-rating upside.
Leverage risk: Assets/equity of 13.74x amplifies downside in adverse scenarios, and absence of cash flow data limits visibility into earnings quality and cash conversion.

Regulatory and macro headwinds: The Trump administration upheld strict merger guidelines (2025-03-09) constraining advisory revenue potential, while tariff-related recession fears (2025-03-10) have pressured bank stocks and could reduce market activity.

Franchise resilience: Goldman Sachs maintains leadership in M&A, equities, and asset management with active deal flow including Chegg strategic review (2025-02-25) and stabilization mandates, partially offsetting near-term headwinds.

ITEM 1.    BUSINESS
Introduction
The Sherwin-Williams Company, founded in 1866 and incorporated in Ohio in 1884, is engaged in the development, manufacture, distribution and sale of paint, coatings and related products to professional, industrial, commercial and retail customers primarily in North and South America with additional operations in the Caribbean region, Europe, Asia and Australia. Our principal executive offices are located at 101 West Prospect Avenue, Cleveland, Ohio 44115-1075, telephone (216) 566-2000. As used in this report, the terms "Sherwin-Williams," "Company," "we", "us" and "our" mean The Sherwin-Williams Company and its consolidated subsidiaries.
Available Information
We make available free of charge on or through our website our Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K and amendments to these reports, as soon as reasonably practicable after we electronically file such material with, or furnish such material to, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). You may access these documents on our Investor Relations website, investors.sherwin.com.
We also make available free of charge on our website our Corporate Governance Guidelines, our Director Independence Standards, our Code of Conduct and the charters of our Audit Committee, our Compensation and Management Development Committee and our Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee. You may access these documents on our Investor Relations website, investors.sherwin.com.
Basis of Reportable Segments
The Company reports its segment information in the same way that management internally organizes its business for assessing performance and making decisions regarding allocation of resources. The Company has three reportable operating segments: Paint Stores Group, Consumer Brands Group and Performance Coatings Group (individually, a Reportable Segment and collectively, the Reportable Segments). The Company reports all other business activities and immaterial operating segments that are not reportable in the Administrative function. For more information about the Reportable Segments, see Note 22 to the consolidated financial statements in Item 8.
Paint Stores Group
Paint Stores Group consisted of 4,773 company-operated specialty paint stores in the United States, Canada and the Caribbean region at December 31, 2024. Each store is engaged in servicing the needs of architectural and industrial paint contractors and do-it-yourself homeowners. These stores market and sell Sherwin-Williams® and other controlled brand architectural paint and coatings, protective and marine products, OEM product finishes and related products. The majority of these products are produced by manufacturing facilities in the Consumer Brands Group. In addition, each store sells select purchased associated products. The loss of any single customer would not have a material adverse effect on the business of this segment.
Consumer Brands Group
The Consumer Brands Group manufactures and distributes a broad portfolio of branded and private-label architectural paint, stains, varnishes, industrial products, wood finishes products, wood preservatives, applicators, corrosion inhibitors, aerosols, caulks and adhesives to retailers, including home centers an
…[truncated 13771 chars]…
unds and experiences. During 2024, we hired approximately 1,500 professionals through our management trainee program as part of our long-term growth initiatives.
We invest in our people by providing learning and employee networking opportunities to drive retention, development and engagement and help employees excel in their current and future roles. During 2024, our employees collectively completed thousands of hours of online and instructor-led courses across a broad range of categories, including leadership, professional skills, technical skills and compliance. We measure our progress toward creating a culture of excellence that empowers employees to learn, grow and achieve their aspirations by conducting periodic pulse surveys and a global engagement survey, which we conducted in 2023 and expect to conduct every other year. We are focused on using these survey results to drive continued progress with our efforts.
Occupational Health and Safety.
Providing safe and healthy working environments for our employees is a core value. We have a consistent focus on Environmental, Health and Safety excellence that promotes employee health and safety, process safety and occupational health, including evaluation and implementation of preventative measures to reduce workplace injuries and illness. We strive for incident-free workplaces and are continuously seeking to improve the programs that are in place to help keep our employees, customers and communities safe, including by regularly re-evaluating our global management systems, standards and performance measures.
Total Rewards.
We prioritize the fair and consistent treatment of our employees in relation to working conditions, wages, benefits, policies and procedures. The Company's policies and programs are designed to respond to the needs of our employees in a manner that provides a safe, professional, efficient and rewarding workplace. Our total rewards programs are designed to offer competitive compensation, comprehensive benefits and other programs to support employees' growth, both personally and professionally, and the needs and well-being of our employees worldwide.
Over the past few years, we have enhanced certain of the Company's benefits and practices to support the health and well-being of our employees. Our enhanced benefits have included paid sick leave, family leave and voluntary leave of absence policies and programs. In 2024, we introduced a new education benefit that offers bachelors' degrees, associates' degrees and certificates for in-demand fields, with tuition fully paid by our Company. More recently, in 2025 we added a new backup child and elder care benefit. We continue to permit remote, alternate and flexible work arrangements where possible to promote increased flexibility and support employee health and well-being, while maintaining our focus on collaboration and engagement.
Regulatory Compliance
For additional information regarding environmental-related matters, see Notes 1, 10 and 19 to the consolidated financial statements in Item 8.
4
Table of Contents

Any statement that is not historical in nature is a forward-looking statement and may be identified by the use of words and phrases such as "anticipate," "aspire," "believe," "could," "estimate," "expect," "goal," "intend," "may," "plan," "potential," "project," "seek," "should," "strive," "target," "will," or "would" or the negative thereof or comparable terminology.
Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on any forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements are necessarily subject to risks, uncertainties and other factors, many of which are outside our control, that could cause actual results to differ materially from such statements and from our historical results, performance and experience. These risks, uncertainties and other factors include such things as:
- general business and economic conditions in the United States and worldwide;
- inflation rates, interest rates, unemployment rates, labor costs, healthcare costs, recessionary conditions, geopolitical conditions, terrorist activity, armed conflicts and wars, public health crises, pandemics, outbreaks of disease and supply chain disruptions;
- shifts in consumer behavior driven by economic downturns in cyclical segments of the economy;
- shortages and increases in the cost of raw materials and energy;
- catastrophic events, adverse weather conditions and natural disasters (including those that may be related to climate change);
- the loss of any of our largest customers;
- increased competition or failure to keep pace with developments in key competitive areas of our business;
- disruptions to our information technology systems, including due to digitization efforts or cybersecurity incidents;
- our ability to attract, retain, develop and progress a qualified global workforce;
- our ability to successfully integrate past and future acquisitions into our existing operations;
- risks and uncertainties associated with our expansion into and our operations in South America, Asia, Europe and other foreign markets;
- policy changes affecting international trade, including import/export restrictions and tariffs;
- our ability to achieve our strategies or expectations relating to sustainability considerations, including as a result of evolving legal, regulatory and other standards, processes and assumptions, the pace of scientific and technological developments, increased costs, the availability of requisite suppliers, energy sources, or financing and changes in carbon markets;
- damage to our business, reputation, image or brands due to negative publicity;
- the infringement or loss of our intellectual property rights or the theft or unauthorized use of our trade secrets or other confidential business information;
- a weakening of global credit markets or changes to our credit ratings;
- our ability to generate cash to service our indebtedness;
- fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates and changing monetary policies;
- our ability to comply with a variety of complex U.S. and non-U.S. laws, rules and regulations;
- increases in tax rates, or changes in tax laws or regulations;
- our ability to comply with numerous, complex and increasingly stringent domestic and foreign health, safety and environmental laws, regulations and requirements;
- our liability related to environmental investigation and remediation activities at some of our currently- and formerly-owned sites;
- the nature, cost, quantity and outcome of pending and future litigation, including lead pigment and lead-based paint litigation; and
- the other risk factors discussed in Item 1A of this Annual Report on Form 10-K and our other reports filed with the SEC.

SHW.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: SHW (as of 2025-04-01)**

**1. Trajectory (Revenue, Earnings, Margins, Cash Flow, Balance Sheet)**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue is $23.10B. The latest quarter (Q4 2024) revenue of $5.30B is down -14.0% QoQ from $6.16B but up +0.9% YoY from $5.25B. TTM net income is $2.68B. Q4 net income is $480.10M, a -40.4% QoQ decline from $806.20M but +34.8% YoY from $356.20M. Q4 EPS diluted is $1.90, down -40.3% QoQ from $3.18 but up +35.7% YoY from $1.40.
*   **Margins:** Latest quarterly gross margin is 48.5% ($2.57B gross profit on $5.30B revenue). The quarterly operating margin was -3.1% in Q4 2024 (operating income of -$166.60M). TTM operating income is $3.45B. Net margin (from valuation data) is 11.6%.
*   **Cash Flow:** **Cash flow data is unavailable for all periods supplied.**
*   **Balance Sheet:** As of Q4 2024, total debt is $9.23B against total equity of $4.05B, resulting in a debt-to-equity ratio of 2.277. Total liabilities are $19.58B. Note: Data for some prior quarters shows total liabilities as negative values (e.g., -$3.75B in Q2 2024), which is anomalous.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The sharp QoQ decline in operating income to a loss of -$166.60M in Q4 2024, despite modest YoY revenue growth, indicates significant quarterly volatility in operational profitability. The disconnect between a positive net income ($480.10M) and a negative operating income in the latest quarter is noteworthy. **The complete absence of cash flow data prevents any assessment of cash conversion or accruals.**
*   **Balance Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet shows high financial leverage with a debt-to-equity ratio of 2.277. Total equity has increased from $3.72B (Q4 2023) to $4.05B (Q4 2024), while total debt decreased slightly from $9.48B to $9.23B over the same period. The high leverage represents a fixed cost burden.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
*   The current PE diluted is 33.10, situated at the lower end of its 1-year window range of 30.9 to 39.7.
*   The current P/B ratio is 21.90, also near the lower end of its 1-year range of 20.2 to 26.4.
*   Net margin has improved from 10.4% (April 2024) to 11.6% (current). ROE is 66.2%, within the recent range of 0.6 to 0.7 (i.e., 60%-70%).
*   Valuation multiples are moderate versus their recent history, coinciding with an improved net margin.

**4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals**
*   The price closed at $349.19 on 2025-03-31. The 52-week range is $282.09 to $400.42.
*   The quarterly return (last ~63 trading days) is +1.1%. The annual return (last ~252 trading days) is +0.7%.
*   The price path has been volatile: +29.3% in Q2 2024, -10.0% in Q3 2024, -14.4% in Q4 2023/Q1 2024, and +1.1% in the latest quarter.
*   The muted annual return (+0.7%) contrasts with the strong YoY EPS growth (+35.7%), while the near-term price action reflects the severe QoQ earnings deterioration.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Strong YoY growth metrics (EPS +35.7%, net income +34.8%). Improving net margin (from 10.4% to 11.6%). Valuation (PE 33.1, P/B 21.9) is near the lower end of its recent range.
*   **Downside:** Severe QoQ deterioration in core operations (revenue -14.0%, operating income to -$166.60M). High financial leverage (debt/equity = 2.277). **The complete lack of cash flow data is a critical gap for assessing true earnings quality and financial resilience.**

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative profile presents a mixed picture. On one hand, YoY earnings growth is robust and valuation metrics have moderated toward the lower end of their recent ranges. On the other hand, the most recent quarter shows a severe QoQ decline in revenue and a quarterly operating loss, against a backdrop of high financial leverage. The fundamental weakness in the latest period (Q4 2024) is at odds with the strong YoY figures, suggesting possible volatility or a cyclical peak. **The total absence of cash flow data materially limits the ability to judge earnings quality and balance sheet risk.** The risk/reward appears balanced between historical valuation support and recent operational deterioration, pending cash flow clarity.
SHW.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: SHW**  
**Decision Date:** 2025-04-01  
**Evidence Window:** Point-in-time through 2025-03-03  

---

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Sherwin-Williams is a global leader in the development, manufacture, distribution, and sale of paint, coatings, and related products. Its integrated business model is organized into three reportable segments:
- **Paint Stores Group:** A network of 4,773 company-operated stores (U.S., Canada, Caribbean) serving professional, industrial, and retail customers. This segment is the primary channel for architectural coatings and aided by manufacturing in the Consumer Brands Group.
- **Consumer Brands Group:** Manufactures and distributes branded and private-label products to retailers (e.g., home centers) and operates 334 stores in Latin America. It handles significant intersegment transfers (~63% of its sales) and supports global R&D and manufacturing.
- **Performance Coatings Group:** Sells industrial coatings (wood, metal, automotive refinish, protective, marine, coil, packaging) worldwide through its own 324 branches, sales forces, and third-party distributors.

The company emphasizes its extensive portfolio of recognized trademarks (e.g., Sherwin-Williams®, Valspar®, Krylon®) and an integrated model where manufacturing and retail store networks provide competitive advantages in service, technical support, and distribution.

### 2. Dated Recent Developments
- **2025-02-18:** Sherwin-Williams announced the acquisition of BASF's Brazilian architectural paints business, Suvinil, for $1.15 billion. The stated rationale is to strengthen its presence in Brazil and complement its existing operations.
- **2025-02-18 & 2025-02-25:** Industry market reports project significant growth in specialized coatings markets relevant to SHW. The anti-corrosion coatings market is forecast to reach $54.4 billion by 2033 (CAGR 5.4%), and the UV-curable coatings market to reach $20.94 billion by 2032, driven by demand in automotive, electronics, and eco-friendly applications.
- **2025-03-03:** Gabelli Funds hosted a specialty chemicals symposium where companies, likely including SHW, discussed industry themes such as **pricing power and margin recovery**.
- **2025-02-23 & 2025-03-02:** Web context indicates positive market sentiment, with analyst price targets suggesting upside potential and note of recent stock performance gains.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks (from 10-K & News):**
- **Macro & Cost Sensitivity:** Business is exposed to general economic conditions, inflation, and raw material costs derived from petrochemical feedstocks (e.g., propylene). Seasonality (sales skewed to Q2/Q3) can be altered by economic downturns.
- **Acquisition Integration:** The $1.15B Suvinil acquisition (2025-02-18) presents execution and integration risks. The 10-K cautions about risks from past and future acquisitions.
- **Competitive Pressure:** The company faces intense competition across all segments from local, regional, national, and international players.
- **Environmental/Litigation:** Subject to complex environmental regulations and is involved in ongoing lead pigment and lead-based paint litigation. The 10-K lists these as forward-looking risk factors.
- **Global Operations:** Exposed to risks in international markets, including foreign currency fluctuations, geopolitical instability, and tariffs.

**Offsets:**
- **Scale and Diversification:** The integrated manufacturing and distribution model across multiple segments and geographies provides operational and commercial resilience.
- **Strategic Growth Actions:** The 2025 acquisition targets growth in a major emerging market (Brazil), aligning with a strategy of geographic and brand expansion.
- **Market Tailwinds:** Positive secular trends in key end markets (eco-friendly coatings, infrastructure, industrial applications) support long-term demand.
- **Brand Portfolio & Distribution:** Ownership of leading consumer and professional brands (e.g., Valspar®, Sherwin-Williams®) and a vast store network create significant barriers to entry and pricing power.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.** The franchise demonstrates strength through a deeply integrated and scaled global business model with powerful brand and distribution advantages. Recent narrative is dominated by a significant, strategic acquisition in Brazil (2025-02-18) to expand in a key growth market and supported by favorable forecasts for relevant specialty coatings markets. While the 10-K outlines inherent risks around commodities, competition, acquisition integration, and regulation, the operational structure and proactive capital allocation (M&A) are clear positive offsets. The absence of negative company-specific news or regulatory actions within the evidence window further supports the view.

---
**Citation Note:** All facts and assessments are derived solely from the provided texts dated between 2025-02-10 and 2025-03-03.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Sherwin-Williams presents a balanced risk/reward profile: strong YoY earnings growth (+35.7% EPS) and valuation near the lower end of its range (PE 33.1 vs. 30.9-39.7) are offset by severe Q4 2024 operational deterioration (operating loss of -$166.6M, revenue -14.0% QoQ) and high leverage (debt/equity 2.277). The complete absence of cash flow data materially limits conviction in earnings quality.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Sherwin-Williams presents a balanced risk/reward profile: strong YoY earnings growth (+35.7% EPS) and valuation near the lower end of its range (PE 33.1 vs. 30.9-39.7) are offset by severe Q4 2024 operational deterioration (operating loss of -$166.6M, revenue -14.0% QoQ) and high leverage (debt/equity 2.277). The complete absence of cash flow data materially limits conviction in earnings quality.
key_factors: YoY EPS growth of +35.7% and net income growth of +34.8% demonstrate underlying earnings momentum, while PE of 33.1 sits at the lower end of its 1-year range (30.9-39.7), suggesting moderate valuation support.

Q4 2024 showed severe QoQ deterioration: revenue fell -14.0% to $5.30B and operating income turned negative at -$166.6M, indicating significant quarterly volatility that contrasts with the strong YoY figures.

High financial leverage with debt-to-equity of 2.277 ($9.23B debt vs. $4.05B equity) creates fixed cost burden and limits balance sheet flexibility, particularly concerning given the Q4 operating loss.

The $1.15B Suvinil acquisition announced 2025-02-18 to expand in Brazil represents a strategic growth catalyst but also introduces integration execution risk, while favorable secular trends in specialty coatings markets provide long-term demand support.

 user experience for consumer entertainment and professional visualization applications and create new virtual world and simulation capabilities. Our technologies are instrumental in driving the gaming, design, and creative industries forward, as developers leverage our libraries and algorithms to deliver an optimized experience on our GeForce and NVIDIA RTX platforms. Our computer graphics platforms leverage AI end-to-end, from the developer tools and cloud services to the Tensor Cores included in all RTX-class GPUs. For example, NVIDIA Avatar Cloud Engine, or ACE, is
7
Table of Contents
a suite of technologies that help developers bring digital avatars to life with generative AI, running in the cloud or locally on the PC. GeForce Experience enhances each gamer's experience by optimizing their PC's settings, as well as enabling the recording and sharing of gameplay. Our Studio drivers enhance and accelerate a number of popular creative applications. Omniverse is real-time 3D design collaboration and virtual world simulation software that empowers artists, designers, and creators to connect and collaborate in leading design applications. We also enable interactive graphics applications - such as games, movie and photo editing and design software - to be accessed by almost any device, almost anywhere, through our cloud platforms such as vGPU for enterprise and GeForce NOW for gaming.
Advancing the leading autonomous vehicle platform.
We believe the advent of autonomous vehicles, or AV, and electric vehicles, or EV, is revolutionizing the transportation industry. The algorithms required for autonomous driving - such as perception, localization, and planning - are too complex for legacy hand-coded approaches and will use multiple neural networks instead. Therefore, we provide an AI-based hardware and software solution, designed and implemented from the ground up based on automotive safety standards, for the AV and EV market under the DRIVE brand, which we are bringing to market through our partnerships with automotive OEMs, tier-1 suppliers, and start-ups. Our AV solution also includes the GPU-based hardware required to train the neural networks before their in-vehicle deployment, as well as to re-simulate their operation prior to any over-the-air software updates. We believe our comprehensive, top-to-bottom and end-to-end approach will enable the transportation industry to solve the complex problems arising from the shift to autonomous driving.
Leveraging our intellectual property, or IP.
We believe our IP is a valuable asset that can be accessed by our customers and partners through license and development agreements when they desire to build such capabilities directly into their own products or have us do so through a custom development. Such license and development arrangements can further enhance the reach of our technology.
Sales and Marketing
Our worldwide sales and marketing strategy is key to achieving our objective of providing markets with our high-performance and efficient computing platforms and software. Our sales and marketing teams, located across our global markets, work closely with customers and various industry ecosystems through our partner network. Our partner network incorporates global, regional and specialized CSPs, OEMs, ODMs, ISVs, global system integrators, add-in board manufacturers, or AIBs, distributors, automotive manufacturers and tier-1 automotive suppliers, and other ecosystem participants.
Members of our sales team have technical expertise and product and industry knowledge. We also employ a team of application engineers and solution architects to provide pre-sales assistance to our partner network in designing, testing, and qualifying system designs that incorporate our platforms. For example, our solution architects work with CSPs to provide pre-sales assistance to enable our customers to optimize their hardware and software infrastructure for generative AI and LLM training and deployment. They also work with foundation model and enterprise software developers to enable our customers to optimize the training and fine-tuning of their models and services, and with enterprise end-users, often in collaboration with their global system integrator of choice, to fine-tune models and build AI applications. We believe that the depth and quality of our design support are key to improving our partner network's time-to-market, maintaining a high level of customer satisfaction, and fostering relationships that encourage our customers and partner network to use the next generation of our products within each platform.
To encourage the development of applications optimized for our platforms and software, we seek to establish and maintain strong relationships in the software development community. Engineering and marketing personnel engage with key software developers to promote and discuss our platforms, as well as to ascertain individual product requirements and solve technical problems. Our developer program supports the development of AI frameworks, SDKs, and APIs for software applications and game titles that are optimized for our platforms. Our Deep Learning Institute provides in-person and online training for developers in industries and organizations around the world to build AI and accelerated computing applications that leverage our platforms.
Seasonality
Our computing platforms serve a diverse set of markets such as data centers, gaming, professional visualization, and automotive. Our desktop gaming products typically see stronger revenue in the second half of our fiscal year. Historical seasonality trends may not repeat.
Manufacturing
We utilize a fabless and contracting manufacturing strategy, whereby we employ and partner with key suppliers for all phases of the manufacturing process, including wafer fabrication, assembly, testing, and packaging. We use the expertise of industry-leading suppliers that are certified by the International Organization for Standardization in such areas as fabrication, assembly, quality control and assurance, reliability, and testing. Additionally, we can avoid many of the significant costs and risks associated with owning and operating manufacturing operations. While we may directly procure certain raw materials used in the production of our products, such as memory, substrates, and a variety of components, our suppliers are responsible for procurement of most raw materials used in the production of our products. As a result, we can focus our resources on product design, quality assurance, marketing, and customer support. In periods

Our supply chain is mainly concentrated in the Asia-Pacific region. NVDA.quantitative_report:
**NVDA Quantitative Report – 2025-04-01 (Point-in-Time)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Demonstrated hyper-growth over the reported quarters. Revenue grew from Q1 2023's $7.19B to Q2 2024's $30.04B, an increase of approximately 318%. However, sequential quarterly growth decelerated, from a QoQ increase of ~87.9% (Q1→Q2 2023) to ~15.4% (Q1→Q2 2024).
*   **Earnings & Margins:** Net income grew in tandem, from $2.04B (Q1 2023) to $16.60B (Q2 2024). Margins are exceptionally high and stable: gross margin was 75.0% (Q2 2024), operating margin 62.4%, and net margin 55.8%.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Equity expanded dramatically from $24.52B (Q1 2023) to $79.33B (Q4 2024), a 223% increase. Total debt was reduced from $10.95B to $8.46B. Total assets grew to $111.60B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality Note:** A critical data inconsistency exists. For Q2 2024 (2024-07-28), reported EPS diluted is $0.67 while net income is $16.60B. This is likely due to a forward stock split not reflected in the point-in-time data. Quality assessment based on supplied margins is strong, but the EPS figure requires clarification.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Quantitatively robust. Debt-to-equity is a low 0.107. Total equity ($79.33B) significantly exceeds total liabilities ($32.27B). The absence of recent cash-flow data prevents a full assessment of cash conversion.

**3. Valuation vs. Company History**
*   **Latest Ratios (2025-03-31):** P/E (diluted) = 36.86, P/B = 33.89, P/S = 20.60.
*   **Historical Context (Window: 2024-04-01 to 2025-03-31):** The P/E ranged from a low of 5.8 to a high of 70.0. The current P/E of 36.86 is below the window high but remains historically elevated. The P/E has compressed significantly from a peak of 68.79 (2024-11-14) to the current level. P/B has similarly contracted from a high of 63.8.

**4. Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
*   **Price Level & Returns:** The stock closed at $108.38 on 2025-03-31. The 52-week high is $195.95, low is $75.61.
*   **Recent Momentum:** The stock has declined -22.5% over the most recent quarter (from ~$139.93 on 2024-12-26). Despite this, it has returned +20.1% over the past year (from ~$90.25 on 2024-03-27).
*   **vs. Fundamentals:** The sharp quarterly price decline (-22.5%) has occurred alongside continued reported fundamental strength (high margins, revenue growth). The valuation multiple compression (P/E from ~69 to ~37) has driven this underperformance relative to the profit growth trajectory.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** The company exhibits sustained revenue growth with sector-leading margins (75% gross margin). Balance-sheet equity growth is exceptional, providing a strong foundation. Valuation, while still premium, has compressed from its peak.
*   **Downside:** Revenue growth shows clear sequential deceleration. The high valuation multiples (P/B of 33.89, P/S of 20.60) embed expectations for continued growth and high margins. Any fundamental miss could lead to further multiple contraction. The lack of cash-flow data is a material gap in assessing true earnings quality.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
*   The fundamental trajectory (revenue growth, margin expansion, balance-sheet equity build) has been powerfully positive through the latest reported period.
*   Valuation has undergone significant multiple compression from 2024 peaks but remains elevated on a historical basis (P/E ~37, P/B ~34).
*   The recent -22.5% quarterly price decline represents a reconciliation between the strong past growth trajectory and high future expectations, now priced at a lower multiple.
*   **Conclusion:** The quantitative evidence presents a profile of exceptional fundamental strength whose valuation has been recently reset but remains above historical lows. The risk/reward is now more balanced, contingent on the durability of growth and margins against the newly compressed multiples. The major unknown is cash-flow generation, which is unavailable in this data set.
NVDA.qualitative_report:
### **Qualitative Analysis Report: NVIDIA (NVDA)**
**Decision Date:** 2025-04-01
**Evidence Cutoff:** All evidence is point-in-time prior to 2025-04-01.

---

#### **1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on its 2025-02-26 10-K filing, NVIDIA describes itself as a "full-stack computing infrastructure company" reshaping industry with data-center-scale offerings. Its platform strategy integrates hardware (GPUs, CPUs, DPUs), networking (InfiniBand, Ethernet), and a deep software stack (CUDA, AI Enterprise, domain-specific libraries). This ecosystem creates a high-value, high-switching-cost platform for AI training and inference, data analytics, and scientific computing. The company operates in two segments: **Compute & Networking** (dominated by Data Center, including AI solutions, networking, and automotive platforms) and **Graphics** (gaming, professional visualization, and automotive infotainment). Its franchise is built on parallel processing architecture, a large installed base (5.9 million CUDA developers), and a virtuous cycle of adoption across cloud providers, enterprises, and researchers.

#### **2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Stock & Market Sentiment (Late March 2025):** Multiple articles from 2025-03-31 report significant stock volatility and a sell-off, losing nearly $800 billion in market cap. The decline is attributed to broader market concerns over tariffs, potential recession, and weakening consumer sentiment, rather than company-specific fundamentals.
*   **Potential Customer Win (2025-03-31):** Reports indicate Apple is "in talks with Nvidia for a major server deal," signaling continued large-scale AI infrastructure investment from a key tech giant.
*   **Sector Dynamics (2025-03-31):** CoreWeave's disappointing IPO is noted, but an analyst states its performance was "due to [the] company, not AI sector," suggesting underlying AI demand remains intact.
*   **Upcoming Catalyst (2025-03-27):** A Business Insider article, citing Bank of America, highlights a "key catalyst coming on May 15" that could help the stock recover from its recent slump, though specifics are not provided in the snippet.
*   **Supply Chain & Legal Scrutiny (2025-03-29 & 2025-03-30):** News covers a securities class action lawsuit against **Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR)**. The investigation involves alleged false statements about product issues that "negatively impacted Nvidia's products."
*   **Competitive Landscape (2025-03-30):** An article highlights AMD as a competitor, noting its AI chip growth is slowing while its core CPU business is strong.

#### **3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
**Risks:**
*   **Macro & Cyclicality:** Persistent market narratives (as of 2025-03-31) frame semiconductor stocks as cyclical and sensitive to inflation, tariffs, and recession fears, creating continued stock volatility.
*   **Regulatory & Legal:** NVIDIA has filed a lawsuit against EU antitrust regulators (reported 2025-02-24 & 2025-02-25) over their scrutiny of its acquisition of AI startup Run:ai. This introduces potential regulatory overhang in a key market.
*   **Supply Chain Concentration & Litigation:** The 10-K (2025-02-26) notes supply chain concentration in Asia-Pacific. This risk is concretized by the **MPWR** lawsuit (2025-03-29/30), which alleges quality control issues in a component supplier that damaged Nvidia's products and relationships.
*   **Competitive Execution:** The 10-K acknowledges an "intensely competitive" market. While Nvidia leads, competitors like AMD and custom chips from cloud giants are actively pursuing the AI accelerator market.

**Offsets:**
*   **Secular AI Demand:** Despite short-term volatility, the narrative consistently points to strong, ongoing investment in AI infrastructure by major tech companies (e.g., potential Apple deal on 2025-03-31).
*   **Platform Leadership & Ecosystem:** The 10-K emphasizes a broad, full-stack platform (hardware + software + networking) and a large developer ecosystem (5.9 million CUDA developers), creating deep competitive moats.
*   **Product Cycle:** The 10-K details the **Blackwell architecture** (launched FY2025) for data centers and gaming, representing a major new product cycle across key markets.
*   **Analyst View of Resilience:** One 2025-03-31 article cites the stock's "historical resilience to rebounds" and the strength of its computing and networking segments as reasons for long-term confidence.

#### **4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral-to-Positive.**
The evidence presents a classic conflict between a robust secular growth story and significant near-term headwinds. NVIDIA's franchise, as described in its 10-K, is exceptionally strong and central to the AI infrastructure build-out, with a powerful platform strategy and new product cycles (Blackwell). Recent positive developments include potential customer wins (Apple) and a clear upcoming catalyst (May 15 event). However, the stock faces immediate pressure from macro concerns (tariffs, recession fears), specific legal/regulatory actions (EU lawsuit, MPWR litigation), and general market rotation away from high-growth tech. The qualitative picture is of a leading company in a volatile phase, where strong fundamental positioning is being challenged by broader market sentiment and specific, yet contained, operational and legal risks.

e narrowly focused entities. Across our business segments, we recognize hundreds of competitors worldwide and as we execute our hybrid cloud and AI strategy, we are regularly exposed to new competitors.
Our principal methods of competition are: technology innovation; performance; price; quality; brand; our breadth of capabilities, products and services; talent; client relationships and trust; the ability to deliver business value to clients; and service and support. In order to maintain leadership, we optimize our portfolio with organic and inorganic innovations and effective resource allocation. These investments not only drive current performance but will extend our innovation leadership into the future.
Our breadth and depth of expertise enables us to take different technologies and bring them together to solve the most pressing business issues of our clients. We differentiate from other providers by bringing together incumbency with enterprises, deep expertise in technology, industries and business processes, a broad go-to-market reach including an ecosystem of partners and alliances, and a strong focus on innovation. All of these attributes position IBM for accelerated growth now and prepare us for the next set of business opportunities, such as quantum computing.
1
Table of Contents
Overall, the company is the leader or among the leaders in each of our business segments. A summary of the competitive environment for each business segment is included below:
Software:
The depth, breadth, and innovation of our software offerings, coupled with our global reach, deep industry expertise and research capabilities help differentiate our offerings from our competitors. Our hybrid cloud and AI platforms allow clients to realize their digital and AI transformations across the applications, data, and environments in which they operate. The principal competitors in this segment include: Alphabet (Google), Amazon, BMC, Broadcom, Informatica, Microsoft, Oracle, Salesforce, SAP and Splunk. We also compete with smaller, niche competitors in specific geographic regions or product segments.
Consulting:
Consulting focuses on integrating skills on strategy, experience, technology and operations by domain and industry. Consulting competes in a dynamic market including consulting, systems integration, application development, application management and business process outsourcing services. Our broad-based competitors include: Accenture, Capgemini, India-based service providers, management consulting firms, the consulting practices of public accounting firms, engineering service providers, and many companies that primarily focus on local markets or niche service areas.
Infrastructure:
IBM is well positioned in the growing hybrid cloud infrastructure market, providing on-premises and cloud-based server and storage solutions. We gain advantage and differentiation through investments in higher-value capabilities, including security, scalability, and reliability, designed especially for mission-critical and AI workloads. In addition, we offer a portfolio of life-cycle services for hybrid cloud infrastructure deployment. Our principal competitors include: Dell Technologies, Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (HPE), Intel, NetApp and Pure Storage as well as original device manufacturers (ODMs) who provide systems that are re-branded. Further, cloud service providers are leveraging innovation in technology and service delivery to compete with traditional providers and to offer additional routes to market for server and storage systems.
Financing:
Financing provides client and commercial financing, facilitating IBM clients' acquisition of hardware, software and services. Financing's ability to manage credit and residual value risk generates a competitive advantage for the company. The key competitive factors include: interest rates charged, IT product experience, client service, contract flexibility, ease of doing business, global capabilities and residual values. In client and commercial financing, Financing primarily competes with non-captive financing entities and financial institutions.
Forward-Looking and Cautionary Statements
Certain statements contained in this Form 10-K may constitute "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 ("Reform Act"). Forward-looking statements are based on the company's current assumptions regarding future business and financial performance. These statements by their nature address matters that are uncertain to different degrees. The company may also make forward-looking statements in other reports filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"), in materials delivered to stockholders and in press releases. In addition, the company's representatives may from time to time make oral forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements provide current expectations of future events based on certain assumptions and include any statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact. Words such as "anticipates," "believes," "expects," "estimates," "intends," "plans," "projects," and similar expressions, may identify such forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement in this Form 10-K speaks only as of the date on which it is made. Except as required by law, the company assumes no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements. In accordance with the Reform Act, set forth under Item 1A. "Risk Factors" on pages 3 to 9 are cautionary statements that accompany those forward-looking statements. Readers should carefully review such cautionary statements as they identify certain important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those in the forward-looking statements and from historical trends. Those cautionary statements are not exclusive and are in addition to other factors discussed elsewhere in this Form 10-K, in the company's filings with the SEC or in materials incorporated therein by reference.

IBM.quantitative_report:
## IBM Quantitative Report — 2025-04-01 (PIT ≤ 2025-03-31)

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue** is essentially flat. TTM revenue through Q4 2024 is $62.75B; YoY quarterly growth in Q4 2024 was +1.0% ($17.55B vs $17.38B). Quarterly revenue oscillates seasonally (Q4 peaks), but the 8-quarter range ($14.25B–$17.55B) shows no meaningful structural acceleration.

**Earnings are deteriorating.** YoY diluted EPS fell −12.6% in Q4 2024 ($3.11 vs $3.56). TTM EPS (sum of four quarters) ≈ $6.43 versus the prior-year TTM of ≈$8.13, a decline of roughly 21%. TTM net income is $6.02B, down YoY by −11.4%.

**Margins are mixed and volatile.** Q4 2024 gross margin was strong at 59.5% ($10.44B / $17.55B) and operating margin 18.9% ($3.31B / $17.55B). However, TTM operating margin is only 9.2% ($5.80B / $62.75B) and TTM net margin is 9.6% ($6.02B / $62.75B), dragged down by a Q3 2024 operating loss of −$802M and net loss of −$330M. TTM gross margin is 56.6% ($35.55B / $62.75B).

**Balance sheet: high leverage, modest equity base.** Total equity is $27.39B against total liabilities of $109.78B, giving a liability-to-equity ratio of 4.0x. Equity has expanded from $21.67B (Q1 2023) to $27.39B (+26%), but total assets ($137.18B) imply equity is only 20% of assets.

**Cash flow: entirely unavailable** in the supplied data. No quarterly or annual free-cash-flow figures are provided.

---

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality is poor.** The most striking feature is the extreme quarterly volatility: Q3 2024 reported operating income of −$802M and EPS of −$0.36, sandwiched between Q2 2024 (operating income +$2.22B, EPS $1.96) and Q4 2024 (operating income +$3.31B, EPS $3.11). This 8-quarter swing between −$0.36 and +$3.56 in EPS signals low predictability. TTM operating income of $5.80B is $1.51B below the Q4 2024 annualized run-rate of $13.24B, confirming that three of the last four quarters carried substantially lower profitability than Q4.

**Balance-sheet resilience is limited.** The 4.0x leverage ratio constrains financial flexibility. ROE has compressed from 0.35 (mid-2024) to 0.22 (latest), and net margin from 0.135 to 0.096 over the same window, indicating declining return generation on a thin equity cushion. Absence of cash-flow data prevents assessment of debt-service capacity or free-cash-flow coverage.

---

### 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History

The stock trades at **PE diluted = 38.7x** at the upper end of its 12-month range (18.8x–40.7x). This compares with PE of 23.3x one year prior (2024-04-01) and a trough of 18.8x (2024-05-02). The multiple has expanded by ~66% from the low while trailing EPS has declined ~21%, meaning nearly all price appreciation is multiple expansion, not earnings growth.

**P/B = 8.51x** versus a 12-month range of 6.6x–10.0x; it sits above the median. **P/S = 3.71x** and **earnings yield = 2.6%** (inverse of PE) confirm the stock is priced for substantial forward growth that the current TTM trajectory does not deliver.

Net margin has fallen from 0.132–0.135 to 0.096 and ROE from 0.35 to 0.22 over the same period the multiple expanded—valuation has moved inversely to profitability.

---

### 4. Price Level and Returns Versus Fundamentals

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Latest close (2025-03-31) | $248.66 |
| 52-week high | $266.45 (−6.7% below) |
| 52-week low | $162.62 (+52.9% above) |
| Quarterly return (~63d) | +10.6% |
| Annual return (~252d) | +30.3% |

The quarterly path shows a +30.8% surge in Q2 2024 (Jun–Sep), a near-flat +0.7% Q3, then +10.6% in the latest quarter. The stock's +30.3% annual return vastly exceeds the −12.6% YoY EPS decline and +1.0% revenue growth, implying the rally is entirely driven by multiple re-rating rather than fundamental improvement.

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside case (quantitative):** If Q3 2024's negative quarter proves an anomaly and TTM margins revert toward Q4 2024 levels (operating margin ~19%, net margin ~16.6%), annualized EPS would approach ~$12.4, which at the current $248.66 price would imply a ~20x forward PE—within the historical range. Equity growth (+26% over 8 quarters) also supports a higher P/B if sustained.

**Downside case (quantitative):** The PE of 38.7x sits 1% below the 12-month ceiling of 40.7x, leaving almost no multiple expansion room. A reversion to the 12-month median PE of ~23x on unchanged TTM EPS of ~$6.43 implies a price near $148, representing a −40% drawdown. The combination of declining ROE (from 0.35 to 0.22), declining net margin (from 0.135 to 0.096), a 4.0x leverage ratio, and extreme quarterly earnings volatility all argue for mean-reversion of the multiple. The absence of cash-flow data removes the ability to verify dividend or buyback sustainability.

---

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

IBM presents a **negative quantitative skew** at $248.66. The stock has appreciated +30.3% over the past year while TTM earnings have declined ~12–21% (depending on measure), margins have compressed, and ROE has fallen from 0.35 to 0.22. The PE multiple of 38.7x is near its 12-month ceiling and roughly 2x the 12-month trough of 18.8x. The balance sheet carries 4.0x leverage with no cash-flow data to offset that concern. Q3 2024's operating loss of −$802M illustrates fundamental fragility. The predominant risk is multiple compression on weak or volatile earnings; the path to upside requires sustained earnings acceleration that the supplied TTM trajectory does not yet support.
IBM.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment Report: IBM (as of 2025-04-01)

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2025-02-25 10-K)**
IBM positions itself as a leader in hybrid cloud and AI, helping clients with digital transformation. Its competitive strengths include a broad portfolio of software, consulting, and mission-critical infrastructure, supported by a strong research organization and a global ecosystem of strategic partners (e.g., Adobe, AWS, Microsoft). The company states it is the leader or among the leaders in its four business segments (Software, Consulting, Infrastructure, Financing). Key competitive methods include technology innovation, breadth of capabilities, deep client relationships, and brand trust.

**2. Dated Recent Developments**
*   **Financial Guidance:** As of 2025-02-10 and reconfirmed on 2025-03-02, IBM management has provided 2025 guidance for revenue growth of "at least 5%" on a constant currency basis, driven by portfolio mix, operating leverage, and productivity initiatives. An article from 2025-03-03 references guidance for "revenue growth inflecting up to 5-plus percent" with "adjusted EBITDA growing double digit."
*   **AI & Partnerships:** On 2025-03-19, NVIDIA announced a collaboration with IBM (and others) to drive AI advancements across industries. A web snippet from 2025-02-12 highlights IBM's "optionality" in creating and managing AI applications.
*   **Market Positioning:** On 2025-03-10, an article noted IBM stock had gained ~19% in early 2025, outpacing the S&P 500.
*   **Legal & Regulatory:**
    *   **2025-03-10 / 2025-03-11:** A U.K. High Court ruled against an IBM subsidiary LzLabs, in an intellectual property case. Furthermore, the U.S. Supreme Court sided with IBM, upholding a win in a $1.6 billion software contract dispute with BMC.
    *   **2025-02-20:** IBM responded to overtime lawsuits from U.S. employees with a 15% salary cut for affected roles.
    *   **2025-03-13:** IBM dropped a corporate policy that considered diversity hiring in executive pay calculations, citing legal challenges and pressure.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal and Litigation Overhang.** Significant litigation includes ongoing overtime lawsuits (2025-02-20) and the aftermath of reverse-engineering claims (2025-03-10). While IBM won key rulings, the litigation creates potential financial exposure and reputational uncertainty.
    *   **Offset:** Recent Supreme Court victory (2025-03-11) in a major contract dispute reduces one specific legal overhang.
*   **Risk: Intense Competition & Execution.** IBM operates in highly competitive segments against deep-pocketed rivals (10-K). Success hinges on flawless execution of its hybrid cloud and AI strategy against competitors like Microsoft, Amazon, and Google.
    *   **Offset:** IBM's stated differentiation is its enterprise incumbency, broad capability stack, and strategic partnerships (10-K), which provide a defensible market position.
*   **Risk: Market & Adoption Risks.** The company's growth is tied to the successful adoption of its hybrid cloud and AI platforms by enterprises, which face macroeconomic and budgetary pressures.
    *   **Offset:** Strong, broad guidance for 2025 (5%+ revenue growth) suggests management confidence in near-term demand. Positive market projections for sectors IBM operates in (e.g., hybrid cloud, digital twins, per news on 2025-03-21 and 2025-03-24) support the long-term growth thesis.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral to Slightly Positive.**
The qualitative picture is balanced. The company has a well-defined franchise focused on high-growth areas (hybrid cloud, AI) and is delivering strong near-term guidance (2025-02-10, 2025-03-02). Strategic partnerships like that with NVIDIA (2025-03-19) and recent legal victories (2025-03-10, 2025-03-11) are positive catalysts. However, these are offset by the persistent overhang from various lawsuits (2025-02-20) and the inherent execution risk associated with competing against formidable cloud and software rivals. The recent pivot in corporate policy (2025-03-13) introduces a new, albeit contained, reputational variable. Overall, the evidence points to a stable, competently guided company with solid near-term momentum, but not one without meaningful competitive and legal headwinds.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2025-02-14 section=business period_end=2024-12-31
Item 1. Business.
Overview
JPMorgan Chase & Co. ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm", NYSE: JPM), a financial holding company incorporated under Delaware law in 1968, is a leading financial services firm based in the United States of America ("U.S."), with operations worldwide. JPMorganChase had $4.0 trillion in assets and $344.8 billion in stockholders' equity as of December 31, 2024. The Firm is a leader in investment banking, financial services for consumers and small businesses, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management. Under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands, the Firm serves millions of customers, predominantly in the U.S., and many of the world's most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients globally.
JPMorganChase's principal bank subsidiary is JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association ("JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A."), a national banking association with U.S. branches in 48 states and Washington, D.C. JPMorganChase's principal non-bank subsidiary is J.P. Morgan Securities LLC ("J.P. Morgan Securities"), a U.S. broker-dealer. The bank and non-bank subsidiaries of JPMorganChase operate nationally as well as through overseas branches and subsidiaries, representative offices and subsidiary foreign banks. The Firm's principal operating subsidiaries outside the U.S. are J.P. Morgan Securities plc and J.P. Morgan SE ("JPMSE"), which are subsidiaries of JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. and are based in the United Kingdom ("U.K.") and Germany, respectively.
The Firm's website is www.jpmorganchase.com. JPMorganChase makes available on its website, free of charge, annual reports on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K pursuant to Section 13(a) or Section 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as soon as reasonably practicable after it electronically files or furnishes such material to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") at www.sec.gov. JPMorganChase makes new and important information about the Firm available on its website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com, including on the Investor Relations section of its website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir. Information on the Firm's website, including documents on the website that are referenced in this Form 10-K, is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024 ("2024 Form 10-K" or "Form 10-K") or the Firm's other filings with the SEC.
Business segments & Corporate
Effective in the second quarter of 2024, JPMorganChase reorganized its reportable business segments by combining the former Corporate & Investment Bank and Commercial Banking business segments to form one reportable segment, the Commercial & Investment Bank. As a result of the reorganization, the Firm has three reportable business segments – Consumer & Community Banking ("CCB"), Commercial & Investment Bank ("CIB") and Asset & Wealth Management ("AWM") – with the remaining activities in Corporate. The Firm's consumer business segment is CCB, and the Firm's wholesale business segments are CIB and AWM.
A description of the Firm's reportable business segments and the products and services that they provide to their respective client bases, as well as a description of Corporate activities, is provided in the Management's discussion and analysis of financial condition and results of operations section of this Form 10-K ("Management's discussion and analysis" or "MD&A") under the heading "Business Segment & Corporate Results," which begins on page 52, and in Note 32.
Competition
JPMorganChase and its subsidiaries and affiliates operate in highly competitive environments. Competitors include other banks, brokerage firms, investment banking companies, merchant banks, hedge funds, commodity trading companies, private equity firms, insurance companies, mutual fund companies, investment managers, credit card companies, mortgage banking companies, trust companies, securities processing companies, automobile financing companies, leasing companies, e-commerce and other internet-based companies, financial technology companies, and other companies engaged in providing similar and new products and services. The Firm's businesses generally compete on the basis of the quality and variety of the Firm's products and services, transaction execution, innovation, reputation and price. Competition also varies based on the types of clients, customers, industries and geographies served. With respect to some of its geographies and products, JPMorganChase competes globally; with respect to others, the Firm competes on a national or regional basis. New competitors in the financial services industry continue to emerge, including firms that offer products and services solely through the internet and non-financial companies that offer products and services that disintermediate traditional banking products and services offered by financial services firms such as JPMorganChase.
